title: Third-Party Actions, Pinning, Dependency Trust, and Workflow CodeQL · Delivery-System Trace: Ci Actions Codeql Policy Enforcement
summary: A workflow uses mutable tags from unknown publishers, executes a transitive action update without review, allows untrusted workflow edits, and has no inventory or emergency revocation path. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci actions codeql policy enforcement increment in action dependency and update policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use workflow schema checks, policy engines, dependency review, secret scanning, and CodeQL for Actions to detect unsafe patterns and exceptions.
Protect workflow and action code with CODEOWNERS, required review, branch rules, environment separation, and constrained bypass.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment

steps.title: Predict Ci Actions Codeql Policy Enforcement · Theory Ci Actions Codeql Policy Enforcement
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory release uses mutable action tags, accepts automated updates without runtime review, and makes an SLSA claim from incomplete provenance. Pin immutable identities, review diffs, verify attestations, and state non-guarantees. Competency probe: Use workflow schema checks, policy engines, dependency review, secret scanning, and CodeQL for Actions to detect unsafe patterns and exceptions.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Ci Actions Codeql Policy Enforcement contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example release uses mutable action tags, accepts automated updates without runtime review, and makes an SLSA claim from incomplete provenance. Pin immutable identities, review diffs, verify attestations, and state non-guarantees. Competency probe: Use workflow schema checks, policy engines, dependency review, secret scanning, and CodeQL for Actions to detect unsafe patterns and exceptions. A defensible solution applies Ci Actions Codeql Policy Enforcement, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to action dependency and update policy.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-worked-gha-actions-dependencies-supply-chain-2
  - competency: ci-worked-gha-actions-dependencies-supply-chain-2
    artifact: action-dependency-policy.yaml
    decision: worked gha actions dependencies supply chain 2
    environment: trusted release tag requiring an environment approval
    fault-injection: route five percent traffic to a revision with a data incompatibility
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked2
    change: |
      allowed-actions: reviewed-publishers
      third-party-pin: full-commit-sha
      version-comment: retained
      nested-inventory: generated
      effective-permissions: reviewed
      workflow-codeowners: delivery-security
      update: automated-pr-with-compat-tests
      analysis: codeql-actions-and-policy
      revocation: rehearsed
    verify: enumerate nested actions resolve immutable SHAs inspect bundled runtime inputs post steps permissions advisories and update evidence
    failure: Reject marketplace trust, mutable major tags, top-level-only inventory, general review assumptions, or static analysis as complete proof.
    repair: Review effective code and authority, pin immutable revisions, protect workflow changes, test updates, enforce policy, and rehearse removal.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Static analysis proves a workflow is safe under every event payload and runner condition. This fails the Ci Actions Codeql Policy Enforcement contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
